hook.text: A slow query holds a lock for 10 seconds. Every other query on that table waits in line. Cascading slowdown.

buildup.text: Databases use locks to prevent concurrent writes from corrupting data. But locks block other operations.

discovery.text: Row-level locks are fine-grained: only the modified rows are locked. Table-level locks block everything.

twist.text: Long transactions hold locks longer. A forgotten BEGIN without COMMIT can lock rows for hours.

climax.text: Monitor lock waits. pg_stat_activity in Postgres shows queries waiting for locks right now.

punchline.text: Short transactions, targeted locks. Don't block the whole table.

quiz.question: Why do long transactions cause performance problems?
options: They hold locks for extended periods, blocking other queries from proceeding
Long transactions use more disk space
The database resets after every long transaction
